title: Rewatching 'Tenet' (at Home) in the Year of Christopher Nolan. Plus: Steve McQueen!
format: Chris Ryan joins Sean and Amanda to rewatch 'Tenet' now that it's streaming, then Steve McQueen stops by to close out 'Small Axe.'

segments.heading: Rewatching 'Tenet' (at Home)
segments.note: Sean Fennessey, Amanda Dobbins, Chris Ryan

films.title: Tenet
films.note: All three rewatched it at home and liked it more the second time. Chris watched on his MacBook with headphones and closed captions on; Amanda says it looked and sounded better on her TV than it had at their drive-in screening months earlier. Dialogue that got lost in theaters lands with captions and a 10-second rewind, making the plot (if not the physics) easier to follow. Favorite scenes: the Freeport heist ('that's a fucking plane, man'), the reverse bungee jump in Mumbai, the opera-house raid, the car chase. They call it the most self-aware Nolan movie yet, with John David Washington's protagonist as a genuine change of pace from Nolan's usual inscrutable leads, and Robert Pattinson's Neil doing a lot of the film's humor.

segments.heading: In conversation
segments.note: 'Small Axe,' his five-part BBC/Amazon anthology (Mangrove, Lovers Rock, Red, White and Blue, Alex Wheatle, Education), wrapped that weekend

films.title: Widows
films.note: McQueen's own film, and the first of his movies he's shown his daughter.

films.title: Cars
films.note: McQueen's son went through a phase of watching it on repeat at bedtime.

films.title: Occupied City
films.note: The documentary he was shooting at the time (referred to here by its working title, 'Occupy City'), on the Nazi occupation of Amsterdam from 1940 to 1945.

films.title: The Rules of the Game
films.note: Rewatched during quarantine. McQueen calls it possibly the first great farce and loves how quickly it moves.

films.title: Five Easy Pieces
films.note: Rewatched during quarantine. He singles out Karen Black's performance and the wordless final scene.

films.title: Some Like It Hot
films.note: Shown to his daughter: his pitch was a black-and-white comedy with a female lead and Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis. She loved it.

films.title: The Apartment
films.note: Also shown to his daughter, on a Billy Wilder kick.

films.title: Chungking Express
films.note: Rounded out the Billy Wilder day with some Wong Kar-wai.

films.title: Blackboard Jungle
films.note: McQueen traces rock and roll's arrival in the UK to this film's use of 'Rock Around the Clock,' with Sidney Poitier and Glenn Ford.

TV: Small Axe: Red, White and Blue
